title: AI Agents — Classics of 2023–2025 — Knowledge Graph
thesis: Between 2023 and 2025, large-language-model agents went from prompting curiosities to working systems that reason, plan, use tools, communicate with one another, and complete real-world tasks across software engineering, the web, and scientific research. This knowledge graph maps the classics of that three-year window, filtered to landmark works (≥ 500 citations) at top AI venues, organised around three coordinated lines of progress — single-agent reasoning + planning + tools + memory; multi-agent collaboration and emergent behaviour; and real-world agentic applications — plus a forward-looking Planning view distilling the field's next 18 months from each paper's own discussion and limitations.

paradigms.summary: The single-agent core: how LLMs are made to reason step by step, plan over horizons longer than one prompt, call external tools, and remember across episodes. The foundational papers of agentic AI.

sentences.text: By integrating 18 expert-designed tools and using GPT-4 as the LLM, ChemCrow augments the LLM performance in chemistry, and new capabilities emerge.

Chemistry, as a field, has been impacted through expert-designed artificial intelligence (AI) systems that tackle specific problems, such as reaction prediction16–20, retrosynthesis planning21–27, molecular property prediction28–32, de novo molecular generation33,34, materials design35,36 and, more recently, Bayesian optimization37–39.

Although most tools are developed by the open-source community or made accessible through application programming interfaces (APIs), their integration and interoperability pose considerable challenges for experimental chemists, mainly due to their lack of computational skill sets and the diversity of tools with steep learning curves, thereby preventing the full exploitation of their potential.

Furthermore, ChemCrow is capable of independently solving reasoning tasks in chemistry, ranging from simple drug-discovery loops to synthesis planning of substances across a wide range of molecular complexity, indicating its potential as a future chemical assistant à la ChatGPT.

Inter- nally, the tool requests a synthesis plan (using the RXNPlanner tool), obtains the action sequence to be executed on the robot and uses a LLM-powered loop to adapt the errors and warnings in the action sequence.

HuggingGPT is a collaborative system for solving AI tasks, composed of a large language model (LLM) and numerous expert models from ML communities.
Following task planning, HuggingGPT proceeds to the task of matching tasks with models, i.e., selecting the most appropriate model for each task in the parsed task list.

We demonstrate that this a) makes the LLM adapt to test-time changes in API documentation, and b) improves performance from in-context learning, and finally c) show that it reduces hallucination error.
Hallucination with LLM One phenomenon we observe is that zero-shot prompting with LLMs (GPT-4/GPT-3.5) to call APIs results in dire hallucination errors.
However, with the introduction of Gorilla’s retriever-aware training, we can readily adapt to changes in API documentation.
Gorilla generates reliable API calls to ML models without hallucination, demonstrates an impressive capability to adapt to test-time API usage changes, and can satisfy constraints while picking APIs.

claims.claim: Reflection / self-refinement loops (Reflexion, Self-Refine, Self-Discover) and persistent memory architectures (MemGPT, Memory-Augmented Transformers) let agents learn from their own mistakes within and across episodes — the bridge from stateless prompting to long-horizon autonomy.

sentences.text: Concretely, Reflexion agents verbally reflect on task feedback signals, then maintain their own reflective text in an episodic memory buffer to induce better decision-making in subsequent trials.
We also conduct ablation and analysis studies using different feedback signals, feedback incorporation methods, and agent types, and provide insights into how they affect performance.
• We explore this emergent property of self-reflection in LLMs and empirically show that self-reflection is extremely useful to learn complex tasks over a handful of trials.
Self-reflection The Self-Reflection model instantiated as an LLM, plays a crucial role in the Reflexion framework by generating verbal self-reflections to provide valuable feedback for future trials.
In the Reflexion runs, the agent uses self-reflection to find its mistake, update its memory, reset the environment, and start a new trial.

evidence_points.text: Self-Discover: Large Language Models Self-Compose : Self-Discover named in claim: composes atomic reasoning modules; 32% gain on BBH with GPT-4.

sentences.text: Core to the framework is a self- discovery process where LLMs select multiple atomic reasoning modules such as critical think- ing and step-by-step thinking, and compose them into an explicit reasoning structure for LLMs to follow during decoding.

Finally, we show that the self-discovered reasoning structures are universally applicable across model families: from PaLM 2-L to GPT-4, and from GPT-4 to Llama2, and share commonal- ities with human reasoning patterns.
First, the discovered reasoning structure is grounded in atomic reasoning modules benefiting from the strengths of multiple reasoning modules in contrast to applying a priori module such as CoT.
SELF-DISCOVER guides LLMs to self-discover and compose atomic reasoning modules into a reasoning structure to solve challenging tasks.
Applying PaLM 2-L Discovered Structures to GPT-4 We first use a PaLM 2-L model to discover the reasoning structures of 4 reasoning tasks.

evidence_points.text: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems: MemGPT named in claim: OS-style virtual context management for unbounded conversations/documents.

sentences.text: MemGPT’s perfor- mance is unaffected by increased context length.

To evaluate the fixed-context baselines against MemGPT past their default context lengths, we truncate the document segments returned by the retriever to fix the same number of documents into the available context.
By contrast, MemGPT is effectively able to make multiple calls to the retriever by querying archival storage, allowing it to scale to larger effective context lengths.
This OS-inspired approach was eval- uated in two domains where existing LLM performance is constrained by finite context lengths: document anal- ysis and conversational agents.
Overall, MemGPT demonstrates that operating system techniques like hierarchical memory management and interrupts can unlock the potential of LLMs even when constrained by fixed context lengths.

paradigms.label: Multi-agent collaboration & emergence
paradigms.summary: When several agents share a goal, debate a problem, or specialise into roles, behaviours emerge that no single agent shows. The classics of 2023–2025 multi-agent systems: AutoGen, MetaGPT, CAMEL, Generative Agents, Voyager.

claims.claim: Role-playing frameworks (CAMEL, AutoGen, MetaGPT) and structured multi-agent debate / critique loops raise solution quality on tasks like coding, scientific reasoning, and decision-making by forcing models to surface and resolve disagreement.

useful_outcomes.rationale: CAMEL is one of the three frameworks named in the claim; it introduces inception-prompting-based role-playing between AI user and AI assistant agents to autonomously cooperate on tasks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: The role-playing prompt template and released conversational corpus are directly reusable as the canonical recipe for instantiating a two-role multi-agent system.

plausible.rationale: Comprehensive case studies of instruction-following cooperation across many task types support the role-play hypothesis, though formal head-to-head against single-agent baselines is limited.

scalable.rationale: Inception prompting scales to arbitrary role pairs and large conversational datasets, demonstrated by generating extensive role-play data.

how_to_validate.rationale: Releases conversational datasets and task-completion analyses that downstream multi-agent papers reuse as a validation protocol for role-play frameworks.

evidence_points.text: CAMEL: Communicative Agents for "Mind" Exploration: CAMEL is one of the three frameworks named in the claim; it introduces inception-prompting-based role-playing between AI user and AI assistant agents to autonomously cooperate on tasks.

sentences.text: In particular, we conduct com- prehensive studies on instruction-following cooperation in multi-agent settings.

scalable.rationale: Supports arbitrary numbers of agents, mixed human/LLM/tool modes, and nested chats; widely adopted in industry as the de facto multi-agent framework.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reproducible task suites (math, coding, online decision-making) and open code provide a clear validation protocol for role-and-debate setups.

It obtains 3.3× more unique items, travels 2.3× longer distances, and unlocks key tech tree milestones up to 15.3× faster than prior SOTA.

VOYAGER outperforms prior SOTA by obtaining 3.3× more unique items, unlocking key tech tree milestones up to 15.3× faster, and traversing 2.3× longer distances.

sentences.text: We study how vision-language models trained on Internet-scale data can be incorporated directly into end-to-end robotic control to boost generalization and enable emergent semantic reasoning.

Our goal is to enable a single end-to-end trained model to both learn to map robot observations to actions and enjoy the benefits of large-scale pretraining on language and vision-language data from the web.

Our main contribution is RT-2, a family of models derived from fine-tuning large vision-language models trained on web-scale data to directly act as generalizable and semantically aware robotic policies.
Our experiments investigate models with up to 55B parameters trained on Internet data and instruction-annotated robotic trajectories from previous work (Brohan et al., 2022).

The difference between the RT-2 models and the baseline is most pronounced in the various generalization experiments, suggesting that the strength of vision-language-action models lies in transferring more generalizable visual and semantic concepts from their Internet-scale pretraining data.
